title: Towards LLM-Based Failure Localization in Production-Scale Networks

agents.description: Processes monitoring alerts from 11 upstream tools, extracting key information and generating condensed summaries per alert type per device.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Randomly generated alerts based on value distributions of real-world data; ground truth labels produced by GPT-4 and verified with rule-based algorithms and small-scale operator fidelity tests.

agents.description: Analyzes summarized alerts per device for one of 7 predefined anomaly scenarios (e.g., Device Down, Congestion, Flapping) following operator-defined SOPs.

genai_model.finetuning_dataset_description: Randomly combined synthetic alert summaries as inputs; outputs produced by GPT-4 and verified with rule-based algorithms.

agents.description: Consolidates all device anomaly analysis reports to generate failure scores for candidate devices, reflecting the likelihood each device is the error device (Pipeline 1 Stage 1 joint scoring).

Integrated root causing agent (Stage 2) that combines device anomaly reports, network topology (Pipeline 2), and event timeline (Pipeline 3) to generate final ranked failure scores with explanations. Run N=3 times with Rank of Ranks aggregation.
Reflection agent (Agent R) that analyzes diverse reasoning trials from exploration phase, contrasting correct and incorrect trials to extract actionable knowledge for improving root cause prompts.
Knowledge consolidation agent (Agent C) that summarizes and ranks extracted knowledge from all incidents, retaining the six most frequently occurring pieces for prompt augmentation.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: BiAn uses a hierarchical two-stage pipeline. Stage 1 runs three sequential steps in parallel where applicable (alert summary → anomaly analysis → joint scoring) using fine-tuned smaller models for steps 1-2 and a large model for step 3. An entropy-based early stop gate determines whether Stage 2 is triggered. Stage 2 applies a Top-p filter, then runs an integrated root causing agent incorporating topology and timeline pipelines, executed N=3 times with Rank of Ranks aggregation. A separate iterative prompt updating loop uses exploration, reflection, consolidation, and augmentation agents.

multi_agent_coordination_strategy.multi_agent_coordination_strategy_descri: Agents operate in a structured hierarchical pipeline: summarizer agents run in parallel per alert type/device, feeding into parallel anomaly analysis agents, whose outputs are consolidated by a joint scoring agent. A gating mechanism (early stop) conditionally triggers the integrated Stage 2 agent with topology and timeline data. Prompt updating agents (reflection, consolidation, augmentation) operate asynchronously as a continuous improvement loop.

human_integration.human_integration_description: BiAn operates as an assistant to human network operators. It is triggered automatically when incidents bypass self-healing. Operators review BiAn's ranked device output and explanations, selectively validate by checking relevant data sources, and make final mitigation decisions. Operators provide post-incident feedback via satisfaction questionnaires and post-incident reviews, which inform iterative prompt updates. BiAn is integrated into the NOC's on-call platform with output formatting aligned to operator workflows.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Cloud network operations incident management at Alibaba Cloud — specifically root cause analysis and failure (error device) localization in production-scale WAN and data center networks spanning 87 data centers globally.
retrieval.problem_description: Network incidents in large-scale cloud infrastructure require rapid root cause identification and error device localization. Manual investigation by operators is bottlenecked by excessive alert volumes (up to 1 GB per incident), complex spatial/temporal device relationships, high incident load, and time pressure — leading to SLA violations and revenue loss.
retrieval.proposed_solution: BiAn: an LLM-based hierarchical multi-pipeline framework that automates root cause analysis and failure localization. It processes monitoring alerts through three stages — alert summarization, single-device anomaly analysis, and joint scoring — enhanced by network topology and event timeline pipelines. It uses fine-tuned smaller models for simpler tasks, a Rank of Ranks mechanism to reduce LLM output randomness, early stopping for efficiency, and iterative prompt updating for continuous adaptation.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: BiAn was deployed in Alibaba Cloud's production network for 10 months. Evaluation combines A/B testing (operator TTR with/without BiAn), operator satisfaction surveys, analysis of 3 representative real incidents, and extensive offline experiments on 357 real cases from 17 months of incident data including ablation studies, latency benchmarks, fine-tuning microbenchmarks, and cross-model evaluation.

decision_quality_improvement: BiAn achieves 95.5% top-1 accuracy over 357 cases, vs. 86.3% for Hot Device baseline
Top-2 accuracy: 98.6% (BiAn) vs. 88.9% (Hot Device); Top-3: 99.3% vs. 93.0%
In tie cases where Hot Device drops to 70.5%, BiAn maintains 97.1%
Fine-tuned models achieve 98.7% accuracy on alert summary and 98.6% on anomaly analysis, both outperforming default Qwen model by >6%
Prompt updating improves test accuracy from 36.7% to 50.0% on difficult cases after 3 iterations
Top-p filter + Stage 2 integration increases accuracy from 87.2% (Pipeline 1 only) to 95.5%
response_time_impact: End-to-end reasoning completes within 30 seconds, ready before operators come online (~1 minute notification delay)
BiAn reduces time-to-root-causing (TTR) by 20.5% on average; 55.2% reduction for high-risk incidents
Early stop reduces overall processing time to 70.0% with only 0.5% accuracy loss
Fine-tuning smaller models for Steps 1-2 achieves significant latency savings vs. default large model
End-to-end latency is only 15% greater than theoretical sum of parallel components
reliability_metrics: Deployed for 10 months in global production network
Per-incident inference cost averages $0.17 (Pipeline 1) to $0.18 (full BiAn)
API latency average stable at ~5 seconds but individual calls can spike up to 16 seconds

ce_technique_effectiveness.chain-of-thought: Zero-shot CoT used in exploration phase generates intermediate reasoning steps; including explanations reduces score entropy (e.g., {0.5,0.3,0.2}→{0.7,0.2,0.1}) indicating greater confidence without affecting accuracy
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: One-shot prompting used for alert summary and anomaly analysis agents; few-shot prompting applied across agents to improve instruction following
ce_technique_effectiveness.iterative-reasoning (Rank of Ranks): Running integrated reasoning 3 times and averaging ranks reduces output variance; increasing from 3 to 5 runs yields diminishing returns

failure_modes: Multi-device failures: BiAn assumes single error device per incident; struggles when multiple devices are equally responsible
Upstream monitor quality: invalid or ambiguous monitor data degrades localization accuracy
Link-based incidents not yet supported; only device-based incidents covered
High-risk incidents with many concurrent anomalies increase noise, reducing accuracy below 90%
Prompt length degradation: excessive prompt augmentation eventually hurts LLM comprehension
LLM prompt obedience inconsistency: agents sometimes skip steps or produce overly verbose output
limitations: Assumes single error device per incident; multi-device failures are not fully addressed
Dependent on upstream monitoring system quality; silent packet drops generating no alerts are out of scope
Link-based incident investigation not yet implemented
Cross-industry generalizability limited; designed for Alibaba Cloud's specific network infrastructure
API latency fluctuation impacts latency-sensitive deployments
Prompt updating has diminishing returns due to growing prompt length eventually outweighing knowledge gains
Candidate device pre-selection (fixed at 6) misses error device ~2% of the time (improved to ~1% with dynamic selection)

baseline_methods: Hot Device (device with the most associated alerts, adapted from 007)
RCACopilot (variant outputting coarser-grained root cause categories)
identified_research_gaps: Handling multi-device failures where multiple devices contribute equally as root causes
Extending BiAn to support link-based incidents in addition to device-based incidents
Validating consistency between failure scores and explanations
Developing more adaptive multi-agent behaviors (retrying failed analysis, skipping irrelevant steps, dynamically invoking other agents)
Managing prompt length growth in prompt updating to prevent performance degradation
Addressing API latency fluctuations for latency-sensitive inference tasks
Improving dynamic candidate device selection mechanism for better coverage
Handling cases where upstream monitors generate invalid or ambiguous data
